back_translation: One day, Naomi the mother-in-law of Ruth said to her, "My child, should I not seek a place of shelter for you, so that you may be well? Is that not so?"
Now, is not Boaz, with whose female servants you have been working, our relative? Tonight he is going to winnow barley at the threshing floor.

Therefore, you shall bathe yourself, anoint yourself with perfume, put on your best clothes, and go down to the threshing floor. But do not let the man know you are there until he has finished eating and drinking.
When he lies down, take note of the place where he sleeps. Then go in, uncover the covering on his feet, and lie down. He himself will tell you what you should do."
Ruth replied, "Whatever you say, I will do."
So she went down to the threshing floor and did everything her mother-in-law had instructed her.

When Boaz had finished eating and drinking and his heart-mind became cheerful, he went to lie down at the end of the grain pile. Then Ruth secretly approached, uncovered the covering on his feet, and lay down.
At midnight, the man was startled and turned over. Behold, there was a woman lying at his feet!
He asked, "Who are you?" She answered, "I am Ruth, your female servant. Please spread the corner of your cloak over your female servant, for you are a kinsman-redeemer."
Boaz said, "May the LORD bless you, my child. Your steadfast love in this latter act is greater than at the first, for you have not chased after young men, whether rich or poor.
Now, my child, do not be afraid. I will do for you everything you ask, for all the people of my city know that you are a woman of virtue and honor.
Now, even though it is true that I am a kinsman-redeemer, there is still another kinsman-redeemer who is closer than I.

Lodge here tonight. In the morning, if he will redeem you, so be it; let him redeem you. But if he does not desire to redeem you, as long as the LORD lives, I myself will redeem you. Now lie down and rest until morning."
So she lay at his feet until morning, but she got up before anyone could see each other, for Boaz had said, "Do not let anyone know that a woman came to the threshing floor."
He also said, "Bring out the cloak that is on you and hold it up." When she held it, he poured six measures of barley into the cloak and placed it on her, and then he went into the city.
When Ruth returned to her mother-in-law, Naomi asked, "How did it go, my child?" Then she told her mother-in-law everything the man had done for her.
She said, "He gave me these six measures of barley, for he said, 'Do not return to your mother-in-law empty-handed.'"
Naomi then said, "Wait and see, my child, until you know how this matter will turn out, for the man will not rest until he has finished this matter today."
